NextToken.description: If present, indicates that more output is available than is included in the current response. Use this value in the NextToken request parameter in a next call to the operation to get more output. You can repeat this until the NextToken response element returns null.

ApprovalTeams.description: An array of ListApprovalTeamsResponseApprovalTeam objects. Contains details for approval teams.

ApprovalStrategy.description: An ApprovalStrategyResponse object. Contains details for how an approval team grants approval.

MofN.description: Minimum number of approvals (M) required for a total number of approvers (N).

MinApprovalsRequired.description: Minimum number of approvals (M) required for a total number of approvers (N).

NumberOfApprovers.description: Total number of approvers in the team.

Status.description: Status for the team. For more information, see Team health in the Multi-party approval User Guide.

StatusCode.description: Status code for the team. For more information, see Team health in the Multi-party approval User Guide.

StatusMessage.description: Message describing the status for the team.

NextToken.description: If present, indicates that more output is available than is included in the current response. Use this value in the NextToken request parameter in a next call to the operation to get more output. You can repeat this until the NextToken response element returns null.

IdentitySourceType.description: The type of resource that provided identities to the identity source. For example, an IAM Identity Center instance.

IdentitySourceParameters.description: A IdentitySourceParametersForList object. Contains details for the resource that provides identities to the identity source. For example, an IAM Identity Center instance.

InstanceArn.description: Amazon Resource Name (ARN) for the IAM Identity Center instance.

ApprovalPortalUrl.description: URL for the approval portal associated with the IAM Identity Center instance.

Region.description: Amazon Web Services Region where the IAM Identity Center instance is located.

IdentitySourceArn.description: Amazon Resource Name (ARN) for the identity source.

CreationTime.description: Timestamp when the identity source was created.

Status.description: Status for the identity source. For example, if the identity source is ACTIVE.

StatusMessage.description: Message describing the status for the identity source.

NextToken.description: If present, indicates that more output is available than is included in the current response. Use this value in the NextToken request parameter in a next call to the operation to get more output. You can repeat this until the NextToken response element returns null.

Policies.description:
An array of Policy objects. Contains a list of policies that define the permissions for team resources.

The protected operation for a service integration might require specific permissions. For more information, see How other services work with Multi-party approval in the Multi-party approval User Guide.

DefaultVersion.description: Determines if the specified policy is the default for the team.

Arn.description: Amazon Resource Name (ARN) for the team that was created.

Name.description: Name of the team that was created.

VersionId.description: Version ID for the team that was created. When a team is updated, the version ID changes.

IdentitySourceType.description: The type of resource that provided identities to the identity source. For example, an IAM Identity Center instance.

IdentitySourceArn.description: Amazon Resource Name (ARN) for the identity source that was created.

CreationTime.description: Timestamp when the identity source was created.

ApprovalStrategy.description: An ApprovalStrategyResponse object. Contains details for how the team grants approval.

NumberOfApprovers.description: Total number of approvers in the team.

Approvers.description: An array of GetApprovalTeamResponseApprover objects. Contains details for the approvers in the team.

Status.description: Status for the team. For more information, see Team health in the Multi-party approval User Guide.

StatusCode.description: Status code for the approval team. For more information, see Team health in the Multi-party approval User Guide.

StatusMessage.description: Message describing the status for the team.

Policies.description:
An array of PolicyReference objects. Contains a list of policies that define the permissions for team resources.

The protected operation for a service integration might require specific permissions. For more information, see How other services work with Multi-party approval in the Multi-party approval User Guide.

LastUpdateTime.description: Timestamp when the team was last updated.

PendingUpdate.description: A PendingUpdate object. Contains details for the pending updates for the team, if applicable.

ResponseTime.description: Timestamp when the approver responded to an approval team invitation.

PrimaryIdentitySourceArn.description: Amazon Resource Name (ARN) for the identity source. The identity source manages the user authentication for approvers.

PrimaryIdentityStatus.description: Status for the identity source. For example, if an approver has accepted a team invitation with a user authentication method managed by the identity source.

IdentitySourceType.description: The type of resource that provided identities to the identity source. For example, an IAM Identity Center instance.

IdentitySourceParameters.description: A IdentitySourceParameters object. Contains details for the resource that provides identities to the identity source. For example, an IAM Identity Center instance.

IdentitySourceArn.description: Amazon Resource Name (ARN) for the identity source.

CreationTime.description: Timestamp when the identity source was created.

Status.description: Status for the identity source. For example, if the identity source is ACTIVE.

StatusMessage.description: Message describing the status for the identity source.

InstanceArn.description: Amazon Resource Name (ARN) for the IAM Identity Center instance.

ApprovalPortalUrl.description: URL for the approval portal associated with the IAM Identity Center instance.

Region.description: Amazon Web Services Region where the IAM Identity Center instance is located.

PolicyVersion.description:
A PolicyVersion object. Contains details for the version of the policy. Policies define the permissions for team resources.

The protected operation for a service integration might require specific permissions. For more information, see How other services work with Multi-party approval in the Multi-party approval User Guide.

IsDefault.description: Determines if the specified policy is the default for the team.

Status.description: Status for the policy. For example, if the policy is attachable or deprecated.

LastUpdatedTime.description: Timestamp when the policy was last updated.

PolicyVersionArn.description: Amazon Resource Name (ARN) for the policy version.

PolicyDocument.description: Document that contains the contents for the policy.

ApprovalTeamArn.description: Amazon Resource Name (ARN) for the approval team.

ProtectedResourceArn.description: Amazon Resource Name (ARN) for the protected operation.

ApprovalStrategy.description: An ApprovalStrategyResponse object. Contains details for how the team grants approval

NumberOfApprovers.description: Total number of approvers in the session.

Status.description: Status for the session. For example, if the team has approved the requested operation.

ExecutionStatus.description: Status for the protected operation. For example, if the operation is PENDING.

RequesterServicePrincipal.description: Service principal for the service associated with the protected operation.

RequesterPrincipalArn.description: IAM principal that made the operation request.

RequesterAccountId.description: ID for the account that made the operation request.

RequesterRegion.description: Amazon Web Services Region where the operation request originated.

RequesterComment.description: Message from the account that made the operation request

ActionCompletionStrategy.description: Strategy for executing the protected operation. AUTO_COMPLETION_UPON_APPROVAL means the operation is automatically executed using the requester's permissions, if approved.

ApproverResponses.description: An array of GetSessionResponseApproverResponse objects. Contains details for approver responses in the session.

IdentitySourceArn.description: Amazon Resource Name (ARN) for the identity source. The identity source manages the user authentication for approvers.

IdentityId.description: ID for the identity source. The identity source manages the user authentication for approvers.

ResponseTime.description: Timestamp when a approver responded to the operation request.

NextToken.description: If present, indicates that more output is available than is included in the current response. Use this value in the NextToken request parameter in a next call to the operation to get more output. You can repeat this until the NextToken response element returns null.

PolicyVersions.description:
An array of PolicyVersionSummary objects. Contains details for the version of the policies that define the permissions for team resources.

The protected operation for a service integration might require specific permissions. For more information, see How other services work with Multi-party approval in the Multi-party approval User Guide.

IsDefault.description: Determines if the specified policy is the default for the team.

Status.description: Status for the policy. For example, if the policy is attachable or deprecated.

LastUpdatedTime.description: Timestamp when the policy was last updated.

NextToken.description: If present, indicates that more output is available than is included in the current response. Use this value in the NextToken request parameter in a next call to the operation to get more output. You can repeat this until the NextToken response element returns null.

ResourcePolicies.description: An array of ListResourcePoliciesResponseResourcePolicy objects. Contains details about the policy for the resource.

NextToken.description: If present, indicates that more output is available than is included in the current response. Use this value in the NextToken request parameter in a next call to the operation to get more output. You can repeat this until the NextToken response element returns null.

Sessions.description: An array of ListSessionsResponseSession objects. Contains details for the sessions.

ApprovalTeamArn.description: Amazon Resource Name (ARN) for the approval team.

ProtectedResourceArn.description: Amazon Resource Name (ARN) for the protected operation.

RequesterServicePrincipal.description: Service principal for the service associated with the protected operation.

RequesterPrincipalArn.description: IAM principal that made the operation request.

RequesterRegion.description: Amazon Web Services Region where the operation request originated.

RequesterAccountId.description: ID for the account that made the operation request.

Status.description: Status for the protected operation. For example, if the operation is PENDING.

ActionCompletionStrategy.description: Strategy for executing the protected operation. AUTO_COMPLETION_UPON_APPROVAL means the operation is executed automatically using the requester's permissions, if approved.

DeletionCompletionTime.description: Timestamp when the deletion process is scheduled to complete.

DeletionStartTime.description: Timestamp when the deletion process was initiated.

VersionId.description: Version ID for the team that was created. When an approval team is updated, the version ID changes.
